title: The Benefits of Meditation
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The (0) popularity of meditation has grown considerably in recent years. Many people find that regular practice reduces (17) anxiety and helps manage the (18) pressures of daily life. Sessions can improve (19) concentration and sharpen mental focus, making difficult tasks feel more (20) manageable.||Research shows that meditation promotes (21) emotional wellbeing by encouraging more (22) thoughtful responses to stress. Practitioners report greater (23) patience and a stronger sense of inner (24) resilience when facing life's challenges. As a result, doctors and psychologists increasingly recommend mindfulness-based techniques to patients with a range of mental health concerns.

example.text: The (0) POPULARITY of meditation has grown considerably in recent years. (POPULAR)

questions.explanation: The noun 'anxiety' is formed from 'anxious' by replacing '-ious' with '-iety'.
The plural noun 'pressures' is formed from 'press' by adding the suffix '-ure' and '-s'.
The noun 'concentration' is formed from 'concentrate' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
The adjective 'manageable' is formed from 'manage' by adding the suffix '-able'.
The adjective 'emotional' is formed from 'emotion' by adding the suffix '-al'.
The adjective 'thoughtful' is formed from 'thought' by adding the suffix '-ful'.
The noun 'patience' is formed from 'patient' by replacing '-ent' with '-ence'.
The noun 'resilience' is formed from 'resilient' by replacing '-ent' with '-ence'.
